summary: Documented and semantically renamed Light Gun, Light Gun beam, and Light Gun ray item variables and local motion callbacks. The PR mainly replaced anonymous offset-style names such as x0, xDF8, xDFC, xDE0, and xC with purpose-oriented names such as timer, angle0, angle1, position1, pos, lifetime, and max_scale, while keeping corresponding local symbols in config/GALE01/symbols.txt synchronized.
what_changed: Renamed Light Gun item state callbacks in itlgun.c from address-derived static names to motion-indexed callback names: motion0_Anim, motion0_Phys, motion0_Coll, motion1_Anim, motion1_Phys, motion1_Coll, motion2_Anim, motion2_Phys, motion3_Anim, motion3_Phys, motion4_Phys, motion5_Anim, motion5_Phys, and motion5_Coll.
Updated config/GALE01/symbols.txt to match the new local static function names for the Light Gun motion callbacks at 0x8028E828 through 0x8028EB34.
Changed ItLGunAttr in itCommonItems.h from separate float fields x4, x8, xC to a Vec3 pos field, and updated itlgun.c to read da->pos.x/y/z.
Renamed ItLGunVars.x0 to ItLGunVars.timer, including uses that initialize the timer to 0, set it to 40, decrement it in motion3_Anim, and return it from it_8026C1D4.

Documented ItLGunBeamAttr fields with comments: x0 as lifetime and x4/x8/xC/x10 as position/angle calculation parameters, while keeping unresolved field names where the PR did not establish stronger names.

Renamed ItLGunBeamVars fields from raw offsets to more descriptive names: xDD4 to position0, xDE0 to position1, xDEC to position2, xDF8 to angle0, xDFC to angle1, and xE00 to lifetime; xE04 remained xE04.
Updated itlgunbeam.c to use the renamed beam fields throughout angle normalization, atan2f/lbVector_Angle calculations, spawn initialization, velocity setup, and lifetime setup.
Renamed ItLGunRayAttr.x8 to max_scale and removed the previously listed xC field from the visible struct definition in the diff excerpt.
Renamed ItLGunRayVars.xC to pos and updated itlgunray.c stores from spawn.pos and ip->pos to use lgunray.pos.
smart_moves: Kept address comments on local static function declarations while replacing the symbol names, preserving traceability back to the binary addresses.
Named Light Gun callbacks from their role in the ItemStateTable rather than guessing high-level behavior, using the safe pattern motionN_Anim, motionN_Phys, and motionN_Coll.

Only promoted fields to semantic names where usage supported it: timer is decremented and compared to zero, lifetime is passed to item lifetime setup, max_scale clamps ray scale, and pos/position fields are used as Vec3 positions.
Retained uncertain beam attribute names as x0/x4/x8/xC/x10 with explanatory comments instead of overclaiming meanings not fully proven by the diff.
Added offset comments in ItLGunBeamVars and ItLGunRayVars, including ip-relative offsets like ip+DD4, which helps future work connect union item variables to Item storage layout.
Synchronized source-level static renames with symbols.txt, avoiding symbol-map drift after local function renaming.
decomp_lessons: For item state tables, motion-indexed callback names are useful interim names when exact state semantics are unknown; they communicate callback role without inventing behavior.
When a sequence of adjacent floats is consistently copied into x/y/z, replacing them with Vec3 can clarify the struct while reducing anonymous offset usage.
A field repeatedly decremented as a countdown and returned by a query function is a strong candidate for timer, even if the surrounding item behavior is not fully named.
Clamp logic is strong evidence for a max_* name: the ray attribute x8 became max_scale because scale is capped against it.

Angle normalization loops bounded by +/-pi and uses in sinf, cosf, atan2f, and lbVector_Angle provide evidence for angle field names, but numbered angle0/angle1 is safer than more specific names when exact roles are uncertain.
Preserve raw offset comments alongside improved field names for item variable unions; future decomp work often needs both semantic readability and byte-layout anchors.
Do not rename every unknown field aggressively. Leaving xE04 and beam attr x4/x8/xC/x10 partially anonymous with comments is preferable to unsupported semantic claims.
naming_conventions: Local item state callbacks can use motionN_Anim, motionN_Phys, and motionN_Coll when the state index and callback type are known but the gameplay state name is not.
Use lower_snake_case for struct fields such as max_scale and timer.
Use pos for a Vec3 position field when the field stores an item's spawn or current position.
Use position0, position1, and position2 as neutral numbered names when multiple Vec3 fields are position/vector-like but their precise gameplay roles are not yet established.
Use angle0 and angle1 as conservative names for float fields proven to participate in angle math but not yet individually characterized.
Keep unresolved fields in xOffset-style form when evidence is insufficient, as with xE04 and several ItLGunBeamAttr fields.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: This PR appears to be a source/documentation rename cleanup rather than a matching-code rewrite; no review text or diff evidence indicates new assembly tricks.
Renaming static functions required updating config/GALE01/symbols.txt entries for the same addresses and sizes, maintaining symbol-map consistency.
The diff preserved function signatures and apparent control flow while changing identifiers, which is the safe approach for documentation-only decomp improvements.
Address comments in declarations, such as /* 28E828 */, remain useful after renaming because they map named callbacks back to their original text addresses.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR slice.
There is no explicit reviewer rationale in the available data; conclusions are based on the title and diff.
follow_up_queries: Check later PRs for more semantic names for Light Gun motion states beyond motion0 through motion5.
Investigate whether ItLGunBeamAttr fields x4, x8, xC, and x10 later receive precise names based on spawn angle/range behavior.
Verify whether ItLGunBeamVars.xE04 is later documented or remains unknown.
Look for additional Light Gun ray documentation around max_scale and ray position usage.
Search for ItemStateTable naming patterns in other item files to reuse motionN_Anim/Phys/Coll interim names consistently.
